clauses.form: SE Mutual Homeowner's Package Policy - Comprehensive Form
clauses.section: Section I, Comprehensive Form, insuring agreement
clauses.verbatim: We insure your dwelling, detached private structures, and your personal property against all risks of direct physical loss or damage subject to the terms, conditions, and exclusions below.
clauses.endorsement_note: This is an all-risks form: coverage is broad, but the Perils Excluded list and any Restriction of Coverage endorsements on the Declaration Page carve coverage back. Check those before relying on this.

clauses.topic: Water damage and sewer or drain backup exclusion
clauses.form: SE Mutual Homeowner's Package Policy - Comprehensive Form
clauses.verbatim: by water unless the loss or damage directly resulted from: a) the sudden and accidental escape of water from within a water main, swimming pool or equipment attached; b) the sudden and accidental escape of water or steam from within a heating, sprinkler, air conditioning or plumbing system, or domestic water containers which is located inside your dwelling or detached private structure; c) water which enters through an opening which has been created suddenly and accidentally by an insured peril; d) water from the accumulation of ice or snow on the roof or eavestrough, which enters the dwelling through the roof as a result of ice damming. But we do not insure loss or damage: a) by continuous or repeated seepage or leakage of water; b) by backing up or escape of water from a sewer or drain, sump or septic tank, eavestrough, or downspout; c) by ground water or rising of the water table; d) by surface waters, unless the water escapes from a watermain or swimming pool.
clauses.endorsement_note: Backing up or escape of water from a sewer or drain, sump or septic can be added back by the Sewer Backup Endorsement EO-1025-0716, page 71, when it is shown on the Declaration Page.

clauses.form: SE Mutual Homeowner's Package Policy - Comprehensive Form
clauses.verbatim: by waves or floods, all whether driven by wind or not;
clauses.endorsement_note: Flood is not added back by the Sewer Backup Endorsement, which expressly excludes flood and coastal flooding.

clauses.topic: Wind-driven rain and storm damage to interior
clauses.form: SE Mutual Homeowner's Package Policy - Comprehensive Form
clauses.verbatim: to the interior of a building or your personal property within a building, caused by windstorm, hail or coincidental rain damage unless the storm first creates an opening in the dwelling or detached private structure;
clauses.endorsement_note: Not modified by an endorsement. If a Restriction of Coverage - Roof Endorsement is on the Declaration Page, roof and resulting interior damage from windstorm or hail are excluded entirely.

clauses.topic: Fungi, mould, wet or dry rot exclusion
clauses.form: SE Mutual Homeowner's Package Policy - Comprehensive Form
clauses.verbatim: by wear, tear, gradual deterioration, inherent vice, latent defect or mechanical breakdown, rust, corrosion, extremes of temperature, wet or dry rot, condensation, dampness of atmosphere, fungi or spore(s) or contamination;
clauses.endorsement_note: There is no separate mould limited-coverage endorsement in this manual. Mould or rot is excluded as a peril; resulting damage from a separately insured peril is assessed on its own.

clauses.topic: By-law, ordinance, and increased cost of construction exclusion
clauses.form: SE Mutual Homeowner's Package Policy - Comprehensive Form
clauses.verbatim: because of increased costs of repair or replacement due to the operation of any law regulating the zoning, demolition, repair or construction of buildings and their related services;
clauses.endorsement_note: These increased costs can be added back by the Building By-Law Coverage Endorsement EO-0600-0113, page 38, when it is shown on the Declaration Page.

clauses.topic: Additional living expense and loss of use
clauses.form: SE Mutual Homeowner's Package Policy - Comprehensive Form
clauses.section: Coverage D, Additional Living Expenses, item 1
clauses.verbatim: ADDITIONAL LIVING EXPENSE: If damage to your dwelling by an insured peril makes it unfit for occupancy and you have to move out while repairs are being made, we insure any necessary increase in living expenses including moving expenses incurred by you, so that your household can maintain its normal standard of living. Payment shall be for the reasonable time required to repair or rebuild your dwelling or, if you permanently relocate, the reasonable time required for your household to settle elsewhere.
clauses.endorsement_note: Coverage D also includes Fair Rental Value, Civil Authority Prohibits Access (30 days), and Emergency Evacuation (30 days or $5,000, whichever is less), all on page 12.

clauses.topic: Basis of claim payment and deductible
clauses.form: SE Mutual Homeowner's Package Policy - Comprehensive Form
clauses.section: Basis of Claim Payment, Section I
clauses.verbatim: Deductible: In any one occurrence, we are responsible only for the amount by which the insured loss or damage exceeds the amount of the deductible shown on the Declaration Page. If one occurrence could lead to the application of more than one deductible, only the largest deductible will apply.
clauses.endorsement_note: The deductible amount itself is on the Declaration Page for the specific policy. Read the Declaration Page before quoting a dollar figure.

clauses.topic: Requirements after loss, proof of loss and duties
clauses.form: SE Mutual Homeowner's Package Policy - Statutory Conditions
clauses.section: Section III, Statutory Condition 6, Requirements After Loss
clauses.verbatim: Upon the occurrence of any loss of or damage to the insured property, the Insured shall, if the loss or damage is covered by the contract, in addition to observing the requirements of conditions 9, 10, and 11: a) forthwith give notice thereof in writing to the Insurer; b) deliver as soon as practicable to the Insurer a proof of loss verified by a statutory declaration, giving a complete inventory of the destroyed and damaged property and showing in detail quantities, costs, actual cash value, and particulars of amount of loss claimed, and stating when and how the loss occurred.
clauses.endorsement_note: Statutory Condition 8 lets the Agent or Broker give notice and make proof of loss if the Insured is absent or unable.
